message:
Welcome to The Unknown Unknown, are you prepared for the challenge?

 *Use `/help` at any time for a list of useful links and commands* 
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
Hello Eugene
yo!
Hey team, getting a few reports now, looks like confusion from the customers on what they ordering?
This is strange
Just seen a couple of reports of customers not receiving the right item on delivery
Hi @bob Can you elaborate on the problem?
Hmm? that sounds strange. @eschulman6-bloomberg.net can you take point and start sizing this up?
Checking reports...
I'm looking at the dashboard and I don't see any obvious issues with HTTP responses, order rates.
:star: Attempt to replicate the issue
One report said the user wanted to buy 'Sunglasses' but he got 'Tank-top' in confirmation
Just double checked with shipping looks like the items that has been ordered are correct and we're sending the right item
Karan is able to place orders.
We have currently got more than 17 complaints so far
We're trying to reproduce
:star: Reproduces the incident
Hi Bob - I can't reproduce -  can you say more about what the customers are seeing?
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
Hi @bez , we may have a potential issue - We are investigating
*Incident Number INC100095:* sev-4
:star: Incident record created
How big is this problem?
Is it a global issue ?
Hi we have reports of 17 users having difficulty with ordering.  Engineers are investigating -  we are trying to reproduce.
:star: Qualify the problem
:star: Formally assign criticality level to the incident
Gathering more information....
@bob what regions are affected?
The customers are complaining that the image is not matching up with the product name
Seems to be that users don't know whether the product they're purchasing matches the image or the description.
I have just reproduced this.

INCIDENT #     :  INC10008
Description    :  The product name and image mismatch impacting Global customers .
Impact Summary :  Customers are able to place orders but Not sure which product they are purchasing as there is mismatch in labels and image
Current Status :  Teams are currently looking into the issue.
Next Update    :  In Next 5 minutes
How is the order metrics at the moment ?
They are going down- we are cuurently invetsigating
Hi @daniel and @shay  what are the recent changes?
:star: Check changes done to the platform
Particularly around ProductCatalogService?
Here's the detailed info on each of the changes made today
Hope this helps:https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/MDI1YjRiNzUxMGEwNGVmMjkwNmU2Y2ZkNzRjZjBjMWE
We'll likely see high return rates from customers, that's going to mean a lot of financial loss from shipping and refund costs.
What is going to be the revenue impact ?
I'm receiving more cancellation/refund requests than new orders. @eschulman6-bloomberg.net What shall we do now?
I'm concerned we might be doing more harm than good by letting the new orders continue...
What is the financial impact?
We'll likely see high return rates from customers, that's going to mean a lot of financial loss from shipping and refund costs
*Incident Number INC100025:* sev-1
Orders are still coming in high volume, and we now have 130+ customers asking to cancel their orders
There would be more damage to revenue by having returns
@daniel Can we enter a maintenance state so we don't take new orders?
Yes, Can we bring th website to maintenance
@hamed  Same question - for a maintenance window - is that possible?
We could try to halt all incoming orders but not sure on the scale of impact
@eschulman6-bloomberg.net Could you please discuss this with Bez?
Yes its possible. Daniel could help you here
@bez
Bring it up
We are confirming with Management
Whenever needed, we can switch the site to maintenance mode via a feature-flag update in the config repo. It cleanly pauses new orders once business gives the go-ahead
Proceed @daniel - confirmed with mgmt
If we're taking the wrong orders, then let's pause all incoming orders right away. We can't afford huge returns or refunds
Please enter a maintenance window now
What is the ETA to setup the state?
What is the lead time to exit the state when we're ready?
Around 1 min
Proceed.
Getting up maintenance banner on the website...
What's the impact?
@bob What is the financial impact while in the maintenance state - no orders coming in?
There are no changes around ProductCatalogueService
Ok the website now displays a maintenance banner
Yes no order
@eschulman6-bloomberg.net How does it look now?
We have now entered the maintenance state - I confirm it.
We have brought the maintenance window now
@eschulman6-bloomberg.net Now that we are under maintenance. Let's prioritize resolution
I can see the banner is up now informing users about the issue. Atleast our phones would be less ringing now
@shay please post all recent changes then
@tanya  FYI
[https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/MDI1YjRiNzUxMGEwNGVmMjkwNmU2Y2ZkNzRjZjBjMWE](https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/MDI1YjRiNzUxMGEwNGVmMjkwNmU2Y2ZkNzRjZjBjMWE)
Hope this helps:https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/MDI1YjRiNzUxMGEwNGVmMjkwNmU2Y2ZkNzRjZjBjMWE
Where are we ? Have we found anything more ?
I'm in . How may i help you
Can we please begin backing out changes https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/MDI1YjRiNzUxMGEwNGVmMjkwNmU2Y2ZkNzRjZjBjMWE#Change-ID%3A-CHG89412
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
And https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/MDI1YjRiNzUxMGEwNGVmMjkwNmU2Y2ZkNzRjZjBjMWE#Change-ID%3A-CHG89418
and https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/MDI1YjRiNzUxMGEwNGVmMjkwNmU2Y2ZkNzRjZjBjMWE#Change-ID%3A-CHG89415
Can I have an ETA for those rollbacks?  @shay @tanya
:star: Manage actions and timings
@shay and @tanya can I have a healthcheck from your areas please?
:star: Form an effective investigation team
Can do it now
Will take 1-2 mins
Same
We are looking into changes and workign with engineer to roll back recent changes
I'm checking infra. @Shay you do the rollback
@maya  please advise if there are any reports of security issues on the system?
Rolled back both the changes. Still same issue
Sure i can take a look now
Checking now...
HTTP reponses have resumed on the dashboard
no memory or cpu spikes all looks good from Infra nothing to highlight
No spikes in WAF blocks or suspicious auth patterns. Traffic looks normal from a security perspective.
Orders are still at zero... as expected.
:star: Use metrics as evidence
There's nothing to highlight from SecOps, no alerts. All security agents are functioning correctly. We don't have any indication of a security issue here.
Although I don't have any data, I strongly believe this issue originates from the application rather than the platform.
It's probably more of a data/application layer issue. But i'll keep monitoring in case anything changes.
What's the status of the rollbacks @tanya and @maya ?
No spikes or response time deviations, app owners must check configuration.
This is not an infra problem. If users are seeing mismatched products, someone could be messing with our system otherwise its an application/service-level bug
Roll back has been done @eschulman6-bloomberg.net
But it's not a platform issue.
I can take a look into our services now
Are we able to healthcheck the application and see whether the mismatch still exists in orders?
Can that be done while the site is offlien?
We don't have any alerts in the system
is offline?
All apps looks healthy, no errors at all
Even i don't see any issue
Checking logs now with Daniel
Must be network or platform issue.
Are there problems with ordering in the UAT system?
UAT works fine - able to place an order there
Do you have any working theories around what may be causing this ?
Got a board meeting in next 15 mins
@hamed Who are the subject matter experts for the cart and order components?
We need Hamed's help here . He's our SME
Can we get confirmation that the database looks correct for items?
:star: Facts over opinion
The issue could be anywhere, we should look at the configuration mapping between product images and names.
@Daniel do you know which application holds this data?
@Tanya,can you check if this is stored in a database or Redis cache?
Rollback didn't work - moving forward with other working theories
@eschulman6-bloomberg.net if there are no visible errors, this might be a configuration issue; the above details may help narrow it down.
:star: Form a working theory
I can check the database and provide a Redis key dump for product:<id>:name keys
Do we have the ability to flush the Redis cache @tanya ?
'Redis key dump' means extracting the stored data for specific Redis keys like 'product:<id>:name' so you can see what values are currently saved in the Redis database.
Ok here is what i found
The configuration is stored in the Product Catalogue Service. I pushed a change in UAT yesterday that updates the Redis cache with the latest product names.
But product_id: 3 is actually 'mug'
@Hamed yes, this information is stored in Redis cache and the cache was refreshed earlier today.
Where are we in the investigation? I just want to know the current working theories
It is, looks like my script has run in PROD and updated the wrong names for each product in DB
Can you send me a photo of what you are seeing wrong with the platform @eschulman6-bloomberg.net
Oh no, that not good! Shall we go with a rollback?
Can we rollback the database?
Is that an option?
If not, can we restore the database from backups?
I think this is cache issue we should clear the cache and restart Product catalogue Service, that will make fresh calls to DB and the cache will be refreshed
No, Dan's query has updated the wrong names in DB. The data is wrong in DB therfore we will have to rehydrate the canonical database with the correct product Id names .
@daniel  @hamed ?
Do we have an ETA for the fix?
We have got the issue - It looks like the Database has been updted with wrong names
@Tanya, i think you are complicating an easy solution. A restart should fix this
It your call @eschulman6-bloomberg.net
Let's follow @tanya suggestion
first
Checking above findings
The database just reflect the correct information, I'd think.
I suspect if a restart would help here
We are currently working on working to update the right information to DB
We will have to follow below steps to Rehydrate the db.
'Rehydrate the db' means restoring or repopulating the main database with accurate data from a reliable source or backup.
1. **Flush** corrupted product-name keys

```bash
redis-cli --scan --pattern 'product:*:name'| xargs redis-cli del```
Whats the ETA ?
2. **Rehydrate** product name cache from canonical DB
3. **Restart** frontend/product-catalog-service** to refresh in memory cache
What is the ETA of this procedure?
4. **Verify** product listings display correct names
No ETA yet
I can perform Step 1 and 2. Dan or Shay has to perform step 3
Around 3-4 minutes
@eschulman6-bloomberg.net please verify that all steps have been completed
Are we good to proceed @eschulman6-bloomberg.net?
Please proceed with this.
Thanks for go ahead, implementing step 1 now
We should be able to recover in 5 minutes
Please let me know as each step is done.
Step 1 is now complete
Proceed to step 2
Moving on to step 2
Step 2 complete
lets proceed with the next 2 steps
Thanks.  Proceed to step 3
Starting step 3.....
Step 3 is complete
All the recovery steps are completed
Thanks.  Can we do the verification, step 4 ?
:star: Cross checking resolution with team
Checking again
Update me once it will be recovered
Can we specifically check Mug, that was problematic before?
Sure
@eschulman6-bloomberg.net Checked the mapping again. And this how it looks now
Always confirm service health, errors, and data testing before removing a maintenance page - it avoids surprises when traffic returns
Can we flush the Redis cache now?
and restart Product Catalog Service as @daniel suggested
Yes i have done it
Still seeing maintenance banner
Given this, does anyone see any reason not to bring up the site?
@daniel @hamed ?
I think we should be good
ok
we haev validated the data and looks fine
Can bring it up
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
Let's proceed to bring up.
Maintenance page removal in progress...
we will bring down the maintenance window
We are LIVE for orders back again!
We've recovered, but a few orders are in dispute. Should we cancel them or contact customers first?
Please confirm this with Bez before we proceed
I see the site back up...
:star: Validate the fix
@Bez we have taken wrong orders during the name mismatch
What do ewe do with those orders?
Looks like orders are correct when I try.
How are the order metrics now?
If a customer really wants to cancel, go ahead - otherwise leave the order as is. For anything not delivered yet, have support double-check with the customer before taking action
I see User Activity and orders have recovered on the dashboard
Looks like we are back to business
Mterics are back
*Incident Number INC10004:* resolve
*Incident Number INC100096:* closed
Well done everyone
Root cause - UAT data refresh script was accidentally run against prod, overwriting product names in DB and Redis cache with test data. Monitoring missed it as health metrics stayed normal.
Thanks @eschulman6-bloomberg.net and team. You kept the Online Boutique on track - great work under pressure

Drill Wrap-Up
